overview: This incredible journey begins with your arrival in Berlin, where you’ll dive into the city's rich history on a guided city tour. From there, you'll travel through Germany, stopping in Wolfsburg and Celle before reaching Hanover, known for its stunning parks and historical landmarks. Next, you'll head to Eisenach, home to the famed Wartburg Castle, and then to Leipzig, a city with a deep musical heritage. After exploring Leipzig, you'll travel to Prague, where you’ll wander through its picturesque Old Town, the majestic Prague Castle, and along the Vltava River. The adventure continues to Budapest, with a stop in Lednice to explore its stunning chateau and gardens. In Budapest, you’ll visit architectural gems like the Buda Castle and Parliament Building. The journey then leads you through Bratislava and Trencin to Krakow, where you'll experience its medieval charm and poignant history, including a visit to the Auschwitz-Birkenau memorial. From Krakow, you’ll head to Warsaw, Poland’s vibrant capital, before traveling to Poznan with its lively Old Town. Finally, you’ll return to Berlin before your departure, bringing this enriching European adventure to a close.

Days in
Berlin

Upon arriving in Berlin, you'll be welcomed by the city's unique blend of history and modernity. After checking into your hotel, you’ll have some time to relax and settle in. Depending on your arrival time, you can take a leisurely walk around the neighborhood, perhaps stopping at a local café or restaurant to sample some delicious German cuisine. Berlin's vibrant energy and eclectic mix of old and new will set the stage for your upcoming adventure. This first day is a chance to unwind, soak in the city's atmosphere, and prepare for a full day of exploration tomorrow as you dive deeper into the heart of Germany's capital.

Immerse yourself in the local culture with a guided panoramic tour of Berlin, the captivating capital of Germany. We’ll see the historic center, Museum Island, the Reichstag, the Brandenburg Gate, the shopping streets and the city’s beautiful parks. Our visit will also include stops at the Holocaust Memorial and the Berlin Wall Memorial Museum. For an optional excursion, you can visit Potsdam and its stunning palaces.

Berlin city tour
Ticket Included
Berlin city tour
Ticket Included
Berlin city tour
Ticket Included
1
Days in
Hanover

We’ll travel to Lower Saxony and visit Wolfsburg, a city founded in 1938 as a planned settlement for the workers of the Volkswagen factory during the Nazi era. The entire city revolves around the German automobile giant. We’ll include admission to Autostadt, the city of automobiles, featuring gardens and exhibitions. You’ll have time for a walk and lunch. We’ll then continue to Celle, where you can stroll through its old town, known for its half-timbered houses from the 16th and 17th centuries, as well as its imposing Renaissance castle. We’ll arrive in Hannover in the mid-afternoon, where you can explore the capital of Lower Saxony, an important economic, cultural, and scientific hub in the country. Its architecture blends historic and modern buildings, along with extensive parks. Accommodation will be here.

MARITIM AIRPORT HOTEL HANNOVER
1
Days in
Eisenach

Begin your adventure in Hanover, the elegant city of royal gardens and grand boulevards, where history and modern life blend seamlessly. Stroll through the enchanting Herrenhausen Gardens, marvel at the striking New Town Hall, and soak in the vibrant atmosphere of Lower Saxony’s capital.


From there, travel south through Germany’s picturesque landscapes—rolling hills, lush forests, and charming half-timbered villages—on your way to the historic town of Eisenach. Nestled at the foot of the Thuringian Forest, Eisenach welcomes you with stories straight out of history books. Explore the legendary Wartburg Castle, a UNESCO World Heritage Site where Martin Luther translated the New Testament, and wander the streets where Johann Sebastian Bach once lived.


This journey from Hanover to Eisenach is not just a passage between two cities—it's a deep dive into Germany’s rich cultural tapestry, filled with royal legacies, revolutionary ideas, and musical genius. Whether you're a history lover, a culture enthusiast, or simply a traveler at heart, this unforgettable tour promises a perfect blend of discovery and inspiration.

1
Days in
Leipzig

We’ll start the day by visiting the impressive Wartburg Castle (tickets included), a UNESCO World Heritage site where Martin Luther translated the New Testament into German. Afterward, in Eisenach, we’ll visit the Automobile Museum (admission included), located in the former main factory of East Germany´s automobile industry, where we’ll see a collection of vintage cars. You’ll then have some free time in the city to explore and enjoy lunch, and if you’re a music lover, you might want to visit the Bach House. Lunch will be included.


Our journey will continue to Gotha, where we’ll admire its grand baroque palace (exterior visit) and charming historic town center. In Erfurt, the capital of Thuringia, we’ll discover a vibrant city featuring a magnificent cathedral and a medieval old town, including a beautiful bridge that has been inhabited for over 500 years.


By late afternoon, we’ll arrive in Leipzig, one of Germany´s most historically and culturally rich cities. Home to famous composers like Bach and Wagner, Leipzig was also a major industrial center during the German Democratic Republic era and today has a population of over half a million.

Leaving Leipzig, we’ll stop at the Monument to the Battle of the Nations, inaugurated in 1913 and standing 91 meters tall, commemorating a famous battle from the Napoleonic Wars. We’ll then continue to Dresden, the capital of Saxony, known for its Baroque and Renaissance architecture, stunning palaces, and scenic promenades along the Elbe River. You’ll have time to enjoy lunch and explore the city.


Later, we’ll cross into the Czech Republic. Arriving in Prague by mid-afternoon, we’ll include a panoramic tour of this beautiful city, often regarded as one of the most enchanting in Europe. Highlights include Prague Castle, the Old Town Square, and the stunning Charles Bridge. To finish the day, we’ll offer an evening transfer to the city center, where you will find many traditional breweries.

A free day to explore Prague at your own pace. Alternatively, you will have the option to explore Artistic Prague, including Mala Strana, the Infant Jesus of Prague, St. Nicholas Church, a cruise on the Vltava River, and the Jewish Quarter.

Budapest

We’ll depart early from Prague and travel to the Moravia region, where we’ll visit Lednice, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. We’ll have time to explore its beautiful gardens and admire its stunning castle. Crossing through Slovakia, we’ll enter Hungary and make a stop in Gyor, the city of four rivers, where we’ll have time for lunch and a leisurely stroll. Our journey will continue to Budapest, arriving in the mid-afternoon. In the evening, we’ll include a transfer to the pedestrian area, where you’ll find charming traditional restaurants, many of them featuring live music.

We’ll include a panoramic tour of the city, divided by the Danube River. We’ll visit Buda, with its historic old town, and Pest, known for its lively commercial districts. You’ll have the opportunity to experience the charm of this magnificent city. The afternoon will be free for you to explore at your leisure.

1
Days in
Krakow

We’ll depart early from Budapest and travel through Slovakia, where we’ll visit Bratislava, the country’s capital on the banks of the Danube. There will be time for a stroll through its historic center, nestled at the foot of the imposing castle.


Our journey will then take us to Trencin, a charming medieval town in western Slovakia, known for its impressive castle and vibrant historic center. There will be time to explore and enjoy lunch.


In the afternoon, we’ll continue our route through picturesque landscapes of forests and rolling hills as we make our way to Poland. We’ll arrive in Krakow at the end of the day.

1
Days in
Warsaw

On your journey from Krakow to Warsaw, you'll make two meaningful stops. First, in Wadowice, the birthplace of Pope John Paul II, you’ll visit the Pope's childhood home, now a museum, and the Basilica of the Presentation of the Blessed Virgin Mary. This peaceful town offers a chance to reflect on the life of a man who left a profound mark on both Poland and the world. Next, you’ll visit the Auschwitz-Birkenau Memorial and Museum, a sobering and powerful site that commemorates the horrors of the Holocaust. Walking through the preserved barracks and exhibitions, you'll gain a deeper understanding of the atrocities that took place there. After these reflective stops, you'll continue your journey to Warsaw, Poland's resilient capital, where you’ll arrive in the evening to settle in and prepare for the next part of your adventure.

1
Days in
Poznan

In the morning, we’ll enjoy a panoramic tour of Warsaw with a local guide. This city of nearly two million people, Poland’s political and economic hub, is both classic and modern, traditional and cosmopolitan, continually reborn. We’ll explore the charming Old Town, with its historic buildings, Royal Palace, and cobblestone streets, all beautifully restored after their destruction in World War II and now recognized as a World Heritage Site. We’ll also visit the iconic Palace of Culture, a skyscraper gifted by the Soviet Union, reflect on the former largest Jewish ghetto in Europe, and stroll through Lazienkowski Park, home to the Chopin Monument. At around 2:30 p.m., after some time for a walk and lunch, we’ll depart for Pomerania until we reach our next stop: Poznan, an active city with a magnificent main square and vibrant atmosphere. You’ll have some time to explore.

As you enjoy your final breakfast, take a moment to reflect on the unforgettable memories made during this incredible journey with Travelxploria. Our representative will be there to assist you with any last-minute needs, ensuring a smooth and hassle-free departure. Though our adventure together comes to an end, the experiences, breathtaking sights, and shared moments will stay with you forever.

Activities
Day 2 -Berlin
Berlin city tour
Ticket Included
Berlin city tour
Ticket Included
Berlin city tour
Ticket Included


Immerse yourself in the rich history and culture of Berlin with Travelxploria, where every moment is made seamless by our experienced tour guide and dedicated on-the-ground support team. Your day kicks off with a guided tour of the historic center, where you’ll marvel at the Museum Island, Reichstag, Brandenburg Gate, and stroll through Berlin’s elegant shopping streets and magnificent parks. Our knowledgeable guide will bring the city's story to life, sharing fascinating insights at the Holocaust Memorial and the Wall Museum, providing a deeper understanding of Berlin’s complex history. With our Travelxploria representative on hand, you’ll feel fully supported throughout the day, ensuring a stress-free experience. After the tour, enjoy some free time to explore the city at your own pace. As evening falls, dive into the vibrant Kantstrasse area, where a variety of international cuisines awaits, making for the perfect end to a memorable day.

Day 7 -Prague
Prague city tour
Ticket Included
Prague city tour
Ticket Included
Prague city tour
Ticket Included

Today, dive into the heart of Europe with Travelxploria as we explore Prague, the Baroque jewel of the continent. From the moment of pick-up, our experienced Travelxploria tour guide and on-the-ground support team will ensure a seamless and enriching experience. Enjoy a comprehensive panoramic tour of the city, where you’ll discover Prague’s most iconic monuments, including the breathtaking Prague Castle (admission included). Our local guide will share captivating stories and historical insights, bringing the city to life. Afterward, unwind with a relaxing boat ride along the Vltava River, offering stunning views of the city’s skyline. In the afternoon, you’ll have free time to wander through Prague’s charming streets at your own pace. Throughout the day, our dedicated Travelxploria representative will be by your side, ensuring you feel supported, with smooth logistics for a stress-free experience

Day 9 -Budapest
Budapest City Tour
Ticket Included
Budapest City Tour
Ticket Included
Budapest City Tour
Ticket Included
Budapest City Tour
Ticket Included
Budapest City Tour
Ticket Included

Embark on a fascinating journey through the city guided by an expert Travelxploria representative who brings history to life. Buda Castle, Fisherman's Bastion, Matthias Church, and St. Stephen’s Basilica. A tour through the heart of Budapest lets you discover both the Buda and Pest sides of the city. The guide will walk you through key historical landmarks, such as the majestic Buda Castle, the stunning Fisherman's Bastion, and the iconic Matthias Church. You’ll also visit the beautiful St. Stephen’s Basilica in Pest, which is home to Hungary’s most revered relic.

Inclusion / Exclusion

Inclusions

  • Assistance on arrival and departure.
  • Accommodation on Twin sharing basis.
  • All Airport - Hotel - Airport transfers on Private basis.
  • All Tours & activities on sharing basis / Pvt

Exclusion

  • Camera Charges.
  • Additional car usage beyond Itinerary.
  • Any supplementary service not mentioned in itinerary as a suggested addition such as water sports or any similar activity.
  • Any other item not specified in "Cost Includes".
  • Additional or incremental expenses arising out of acts of god, political disturbance, strike etc.
  • Any change in government tax structure and amount becoming due thereto.
  • Currency conversion charges.
  • TCS 5%
  • GST 5%
  • Return Airfare

day_details.day_summary:
Begin your adventure in Hanover, the elegant city of royal gardens and grand boulevards, where history and modern life blend seamlessly. Stroll through the enchanting Herrenhausen Gardens, marvel at the striking New Town Hall, and soak in the vibrant atmosphere of Lower Saxony’s capital.


From there, travel south through Germany’s picturesque landscapes—rolling hills, lush forests, and charming half-timbered villages—on your way to the historic town of Eisenach. Nestled at the foot of the Thuringian Forest, Eisenach welcomes you with stories straight out of history books. Explore the legendary Wartburg Castle, a UNESCO World Heritage Site where Martin Luther translated the New Testament, and wander the streets where Johann Sebastian Bach once lived.


This journey from Hanover to Eisenach is not just a passage between two cities—it's a deep dive into Germany’s rich cultural tapestry, filled with royal legacies, revolutionary ideas, and musical genius. Whether you're a history lover, a culture enthusiast, or simply a traveler at heart, this unforgettable tour promises a perfect blend of discovery and inspiration.

4.images: [{"path":"/Hotels/Eisenach/B&B HOTEL Eisenach/1744804861016-B&B HOTEL Eisenach.jpg"},{"path":"/Hotels/Eisenach/B&B HOTEL Eisenach/1744804861124-B&B HOTEL Eisenach 1.jpg"},{"path":"/Hotels/Eisenach/B&B HOTEL Eisenach/1744804861218-B&B HOTEL Eisenach 2.jpg"}]

day_details.day_summary:
We’ll start the day by visiting the impressive Wartburg Castle (tickets included), a UNESCO World Heritage site where Martin Luther translated the New Testament into German. Afterward, in Eisenach, we’ll visit the Automobile Museum (admission included), located in the former main factory of East Germany´s automobile industry, where we’ll see a collection of vintage cars. You’ll then have some free time in the city to explore and enjoy lunch, and if you’re a music lover, you might want to visit the Bach House. Lunch will be included.


Our journey will continue to Gotha, where we’ll admire its grand baroque palace (exterior visit) and charming historic town center. In Erfurt, the capital of Thuringia, we’ll discover a vibrant city featuring a magnificent cathedral and a medieval old town, including a beautiful bridge that has been inhabited for over 500 years.


By late afternoon, we’ll arrive in Leipzig, one of Germany´s most historically and culturally rich cities. Home to famous composers like Bach and Wagner, Leipzig was also a major industrial center during the German Democratic Republic era and today has a population of over half a million.

Leaving Leipzig, we’ll stop at the Monument to the Battle of the Nations, inaugurated in 1913 and standing 91 meters tall, commemorating a famous battle from the Napoleonic Wars. We’ll then continue to Dresden, the capital of Saxony, known for its Baroque and Renaissance architecture, stunning palaces, and scenic promenades along the Elbe River. You’ll have time to enjoy lunch and explore the city.


Later, we’ll cross into the Czech Republic. Arriving in Prague by mid-afternoon, we’ll include a panoramic tour of this beautiful city, often regarded as one of the most enchanting in Europe. Highlights include Prague Castle, the Old Town Square, and the stunning Charles Bridge. To finish the day, we’ll offer an evening transfer to the city center, where you will find many traditional breweries.

A free day to explore Prague at your own pace. Alternatively, you will have the option to explore Artistic Prague, including Mala Strana, the Infant Jesus of Prague, St. Nicholas Church, a cruise on the Vltava River, and the Jewish Quarter.

rowData.a_details: Today, dive into the heart of Europe with Travelxploria as we explore Prague, the Baroque jewel of the continent. From the moment of pick-up, our experienced Travelxploria tour guide and on-the-ground support team will ensure a seamless and enriching experience. Enjoy a comprehensive panoramic tour of the city, where you’ll discover Prague’s most iconic monuments, including the breathtaking Prague Castle (admission included). Our local guide will share captivating stories and historical insights, bringing the city to life. Afterward, unwind with a relaxing boat ride along the Vltava River, offering stunning views of the city’s skyline. In the afternoon, you’ll have free time to wander through Prague’s charming streets at your own pace. Throughout the day, our dedicated Travelxploria representative will be by your side, ensuring you feel supported, with smooth logistics for a stress-free experience

day_details.day_summary: We’ll depart early from Prague and travel to the Moravia region, where we’ll visit Lednice, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. We’ll have time to explore its beautiful gardens and admire its stunning castle. Crossing through Slovakia, we’ll enter Hungary and make a stop in Gyor, the city of four rivers, where we’ll have time for lunch and a leisurely stroll. Our journey will continue to Budapest, arriving in the mid-afternoon. In the evening, we’ll include a transfer to the pedestrian area, where you’ll find charming traditional restaurants, many of them featuring live music.

4.images: [{"path":"/Hotels/Budapest/Danubius Hotel Arena/1741606368031-unnamed - 2025-03-10T170224.430.jpg"},{"path":"/Hotels/Budapest/Danubius Hotel Arena/1741606368107-unnamed - 2025-03-10T170220.811.jpg"},{"path":"/Hotels/Budapest/Danubius Hotel Arena/1741606368146-2021-09-15.jpg"}]

day_details.day_summary: We’ll include a panoramic tour of the city, divided by the Danube River. We’ll visit Buda, with its historic old town, and Pest, known for its lively commercial districts. You’ll have the opportunity to experience the charm of this magnificent city. The afternoon will be free for you to explore at your leisure.

rowData.a_details: Embark on a fascinating journey through the city guided by an expert Travelxploria representative who brings history to life. Buda Castle, Fisherman's Bastion, Matthias Church, and St. Stephen’s Basilica. A tour through the heart of Budapest lets you discover both the Buda and Pest sides of the city. The guide will walk you through key historical landmarks, such as the majestic Buda Castle, the stunning Fisherman's Bastion, and the iconic Matthias Church. You’ll also visit the beautiful St. Stephen’s Basilica in Pest, which is home to Hungary’s most revered relic.

day_details.day_title: Budapest to Krakow via Bratislava & Trencin
day_details.day_summary:
We’ll depart early from Budapest and travel through Slovakia, where we’ll visit Bratislava, the country’s capital on the banks of the Danube. There will be time for a stroll through its historic center, nestled at the foot of the imposing castle.


Our journey will then take us to Trencin, a charming medieval town in western Slovakia, known for its impressive castle and vibrant historic center. There will be time to explore and enjoy lunch.


In the afternoon, we’ll continue our route through picturesque landscapes of forests and rolling hills as we make our way to Poland. We’ll arrive in Krakow at the end of the day.

day_details.day_title: Krakow to Warsaw Enroute Wadowice & Auschwitz
day_details.day_summary: On your journey from Krakow to Warsaw, you'll make two meaningful stops. First, in Wadowice, the birthplace of Pope John Paul II, you’ll visit the Pope's childhood home, now a museum, and the Basilica of the Presentation of the Blessed Virgin Mary. This peaceful town offers a chance to reflect on the life of a man who left a profound mark on both Poland and the world. Next, you’ll visit the Auschwitz-Birkenau Memorial and Museum, a sobering and powerful site that commemorates the horrors of the Holocaust. Walking through the preserved barracks and exhibitions, you'll gain a deeper understanding of the atrocities that took place there. After these reflective stops, you'll continue your journey to Warsaw, Poland's resilient capital, where you’ll arrive in the evening to settle in and prepare for the next part of your adventure.

In the morning, we’ll enjoy a panoramic tour of Warsaw with a local guide. This city of nearly two million people, Poland’s political and economic hub, is both classic and modern, traditional and cosmopolitan, continually reborn. We’ll explore the charming Old Town, with its historic buildings, Royal Palace, and cobblestone streets, all beautifully restored after their destruction in World War II and now recognized as a World Heritage Site. We’ll also visit the iconic Palace of Culture, a skyscraper gifted by the Soviet Union, reflect on the former largest Jewish ghetto in Europe, and stroll through Lazienkowski Park, home to the Chopin Monument. At around 2:30 p.m., after some time for a walk and lunch, we’ll depart for Pomerania until we reach our next stop: Poznan, an active city with a magnificent main square and vibrant atmosphere. You’ll have some time to explore.

day_details.day_summary: We’ll depart early from Poznan, traveling through the vast plains to Germany. We’ll arrive in Berlin.

4.images: [{"path":"/Hotels/Berlin/Mercure Hotel Berlin Tempelhof/1741086329657-unnamed (3).jpg"},{"path":"/Hotels/Berlin/Mercure Hotel Berlin Tempelhof/1741086329737-2024-07-01.jpg"},{"path":"/Hotels/Berlin/Mercure Hotel Berlin Tempelhof/1741086329817-20220531_155750.jpg"}]

day_details.day_summary: As you enjoy your final breakfast, take a moment to reflect on the unforgettable memories made during this incredible journey with Travelxploria. Our representative will be there to assist you with any last-minute needs, ensuring a smooth and hassle-free departure. Though our adventure together comes to an end, the experiences, breathtaking sights, and shared moments will stay with you forever.
